Rotherham.overlayPolicy: mismatch chapters stored under translationOverlays.Rotherham without altering target verse sequence
Rotherham.emptySourceRowsPolicy: empty Rotherham source rows are governed omissions and are not imported as blank text

DRB.label: Original Douay-Rheims Bible
DRB.sourceGovernance: Preserved source versification; active DRB rows 1:1-14 match this source exactly. Active 1:15 intentionally has no DRB row because the preserved source has 14 verses.

KJV.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

NABRE.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

intentionalActiveGaps.reason: The preserved Original Douay-Rheims source for 3 John contains 14 verses; active NRSV/NABRE verse 15 has no DRB source row.

text.KJV: The elder unto the wellbeloved Gaius, whom I love in the truth.
text.NABRE: The Presbyter to the beloved Gaius whom I love in truth.
text.NRSV: The elder to the beloved Gaius, whom I love in truth.
text.DRB: The Senior to Gaius the dearest, whom I love in truth.
text.Rotherham: The elder, unto Gaius the beloved, whom, I, love in truth.

text.KJV: Beloved, I wish above all things that thou mayest prosper and be in health, even as thy soul prospereth.
text.NABRE: Beloved, I hope you are prospering in every respect and are in good health, just as your soul is prospering.
text.NRSV: Beloved, I pray that all may go well with you and that you may be in good health, just as it is well with your soul.
text.DRB: My dearest, concerning all things I make my prayer that thou proceed prosperously, and fare well, as thy soul doth prosperously.
text.Rotherham: Beloved! concerning all things, I pray thou mayest be prospering, and be in health, even as, thy soul, is prospering,

text.KJV: For I rejoiced greatly, when the brethren came and testified of the truth that is in thee, even as thou walkest in the truth.
text.NABRE: I rejoiced greatly when some of the brothers came and testified to how truly you walk in the truth.
text.NRSV: I was overjoyed when some of the friends arrived and testified to your faithfulness to the truth, namely how you walk in the truth.
text.DRB: I was exceeding glad when the brethren came, and gave testimony to thy truth, even as thou walkest in truth.
text.Rotherham: For I rejoiced exceedingly, at brethren coming and bearing witness unto thy truth,—even as, thou, in truth, art walking.

text.KJV: I have no greater joy than to hear that my children walk in truth.
text.NABRE: Nothing gives me greater joy than to hear that my children are walking in the truth.
text.NRSV: I have no greater joy than this, to hear that my children are walking in the truth.
text.DRB: Greater thank have I not of them, than that I may hear my children do walk in truth.
text.Rotherham: I have no, greater, favour than these things, that I should be hearing that, my own children, in the truth, are walking.

text.KJV: Beloved, thou doest faithfully whatsoever thou doest to the brethren, and to strangers;
text.NABRE: Beloved, you are faithful in all you do for the brothers, especially for strangers;
text.NRSV: Beloved, you do faithfully whatever you do for the friends, even though they are strangers to you;
text.DRB: My dearest, thou doest faithfully whatsoever thou workest on the brethren, & that upon strangers.
text.Rotherham: Beloved! a faithful thing, art thou doing, whatsoever thou shalt accomplish for them that are brethren, and withal strangers,—

text.KJV: Which have borne witness of thy charity before the church: whom if thou bring forward on their journey after a godly sort, thou shalt do well:
text.NABRE: they have testified to your love before the church. Please help them in a way worthy of God to continue their journey.
text.NRSV: they have testified to your love before the church. You will do well to send them on in a manner worthy of God;
text.DRB: They have rendered testimony to thy charity in the sight of the Church: whom, thou shalt do well, bringing on their way in manner worthy of God.
text.Rotherham: Who have borne witness to thy love before the assembly: whom thou wilt do, nobly, to set forward worthily of God.

text.KJV: Because that for his name’s sake they went forth, taking nothing of the Gentiles.
text.NABRE: For they have set out for the sake of the Name and are accepting nothing from the pagans.
text.NRSV: for they began their journey for the sake of Christ, accepting no support from non-believers.
text.DRB: For, for his name did they depart, taking nothing of the Gentiles.
text.Rotherham: For, in behalf of The Name, have they gone forth, taking, nothing, from them of the nations.

text.KJV: We therefore ought to receive such, that we might be fellowhelpers to the truth.
text.NABRE: Therefore, we ought to support such persons, so that we may be co-workers in the truth.
text.NRSV: Therefore we ought to support such people, so that we may become co-workers with the truth.
text.DRB: We therefore ought to receive such: that we may be coadjutors of the truth.
text.Rotherham: We, therefore ought to be sustaining such as these, that we may become, fellowworkers, with the truth.

text.KJV: I wrote unto the church: but Diotrephes, who loveth to have the preeminence among them, receiveth us not.
text.NABRE: I wrote to the church, but Diotrephes, who loves to dominate, does not acknowledge us.
text.NRSV: I have written something to the church; but Diotrephes, who likes to put himself first, does not acknowledge our authority.
text.DRB: I had written perhaps to the Church: but he that loveth to bear primacy among them, Diotrepes, doth not receive us.
text.Rotherham: I wrote something unto the assembly; but, he who is fond of taking the first place among them—Diotrephes, doth not make us welcome.

text.KJV: Wherefore, if I come, I will remember his deeds which he doeth, prating against us with malicious words: and not content therewith, neither doth he himself receive the brethren, and forbiddeth them that would, and casteth them out of the church.
text.NABRE: Therefore, if I come, I will draw attention to what he is doing, spreading evil nonsense about us. And not content with that, he will not receive the brothers, hindering those who wish to do so and expelling them from the church.
text.NRSV: So if I come, I will call attention to what he is doing in spreading false charges against us. And not content with those charges, he refuses to welcome the friends, and even prevents those who want to do so and expels them from the church.
text.DRB: For this cause, if I come, I will advertise his works which he doeth: with malicious words chatting against us. And as though these things suffice him not, neither himself doth receive the brethren, and them that do receive, he prohibiteth, and casteth out of the Church.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, if I come, I will bring to remembrance his works which he is doing,—with wicked words, prating against us; and, not content with these, he neither, himself, maketh the brethren welcome, but, them who are minded [to do it], he forbiddeth, and, out of the assembly, doth cast [them].

text.KJV: Beloved, follow not that which is evil, but that which is good. He that doeth good is of God: but he that doeth evil hath not seen God.
text.NABRE: Beloved, do not imitate evil but imitate good. Whoever does what is good is of God; whoever does what is evil has never seen God.
text.NRSV: Beloved, do not imitate what is evil but imitate what is good. Whoever does good is from God; whoever does evil has not seen God.
text.DRB: My dearest, do not imitate evil, but that which is good. He that doeth well, is of God: he that doeth ill, hath not seen God.
text.Rotherham: Beloved! be not thou imitating what is bad, but what is good. He that doeth good, is, of God: he that doeth what is bad, hath not seen God.

text.KJV: Demetrius hath good report of all men, and of the truth itself: yea, and we also bear record; and ye know that our record is true.
text.NABRE: Demetrius receives a good report from all, even from the truth itself. We give our testimonial as well, and you know our testimony is true.
text.NRSV: Everyone has testified favorably about Demetrius, and so has the truth itself. We also testify for him, and you know that our testimony is true.
text.DRB: To Demetrius testimony is given of all, and of the truth it-self, yea and we give testimony: and thou knowest that our testimony is true.
text.Rotherham: Unto Demetrius, hath witness been borne by all and by the truth itself; howbeit, we also, bear witness, and thou knowest that, our witness, is, true.

text.KJV: I had many things to write, but I will not with ink and pen write unto thee:
text.NABRE: I have much to write to you, but I do not wish to write with pen and ink.
text.NRSV: I have much to write to you, but I would rather not write with pen and ink;
text.DRB: I had many things to write unto thee: but I would not by ink and pen write to thee.
text.Rotherham: Many things, had I to write unto thee,—but I am unwilling, with ink and pen, to be writing;

text.KJV: But I trust I shall shortly see thee, and we shall speak face to face. Peace be to thee. Our friends salute thee. Greet the friends by name.
text.NABRE: Instead, I hope to see you soon, when we can talk face to face.
text.NRSV: instead I hope to see you soon, and we will talk together face to face.
text.DRB: But I hope forth-with to see thee, and we will speak mouth to mouth. Peace be to thee. The friends salute thee. Salute the friends by name.
text.Rotherham: I hope, however, straightway, to see thee, and, mouth to mouth, will we talk. Peace be unto thee. The friends salute thee. Salute the friends by name.

text.NABRE: Peace be with you. The friends greet you; greet the friends there each by name.
text.NRSV: Peace to you. The friends send you their greetings. Greet the friends there, each by name.
